---
title: "Wacom doesn't work with tablet pc"
date: 2008-04-30
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by jbm715 on 2008-04-30
So I cannot get my tablet pc to recognize my stylus. I searched the forums but all the tutorials relate to external wacom devices.  Despite that fact I still tried following them to no avail.  Everything worked fine in gutsy :(.

I was hoping someone could tell me what to do to get wacom working again.

Fugitsu Lifebook t4210

Here is my xorg.conf as of now.
```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "stylus"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "stylus"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "eraser"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "eraser"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "cursor"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
	Option 		"SHMConfig" 		"on"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

```

### Post by j0sh3rs on 2008-06-18
I'm having the same exact problem.  I've searched and searched these forums, and most of the HowTo's seem to be for usb or other types of Wacom pen/tablet settings.

I'm also on the Fujitsu t4210, using Hardy.  The system just doesn't recognize the pen as a device, and I have no idea how to make it work.  The T4210 is a Tablet PC, not just a Tablet and Pen, for those curious or unknowing.

Any suggestions from the experts? I've checked, and done, the steps located here:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=765915](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=765915)
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=747500](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=747500)

And have Google'd the issue until I'm blue in the face.

Thanks all.
